answers: Puppetry is the art of bringing inanimate figures to life through manipulation. Types include hand puppets, marionettes (string puppets), rod puppets, shadow puppets, and finger puppets. It's one of the world's oldest performing arts.

Famous puppet shows include Sesame Street, The Muppets, Punch and Judy, and the dark fantasy film puppetry of Jim Henson's work. In South Asia, shadow puppetry (wayang) and rod puppets have rich cultural traditions.

Making a simple hand puppet is easy — use a sock, add button eyes, felt for a mouth, and yarn for hair. More advanced puppets use foam, fabric, and mechanical parts. YouTube has tons of puppet-making tutorials!

Puppetry is a respected art form taught in universities worldwide. It combines sculpture, performance, storytelling, and voice acting. Bangladesh has a tradition of putul naach (puppet dance) that's been performed for centuries.
